# Figure description: Honeyview_regional location plan.jpg

**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type
Tenement/tenure map overlaid on a 1:250,000 scale geological map (GSWA geology). This is a project location and tenement boundary map showing exploration licence boundaries superimposed on regional geology.

## Coverage and Subject
The map covers the **Cuddingwarra Project** area in the **Murchison region of Western Australia**. The geographic extent appears to cover approximately the Meekatharra or Coe map sheet area. Named localities visible include:
- **Redgum Bell** (significant gold mining centre)
- **Cuddingwarra**
- **Great Find**

The tenement boundaries shown include:
- **EL 3000833** (northern tenement block)
- **EL 3000742** (central/main tenement block — largest)
- **E 2003097** (southwestern tenement)
- **P 2003098** (small pegged area, southeastern)

## Key Content
The geological map background displays the typical Murchison greenstone belt geology with:
- **Pink/red units**: Likely granite/granitoid intrusions dominating the western portion
- **Green units**: Mafic/ultramafic greenstone sequences (potential gold/nickel host rocks), concentrated in linear north-northwest trending belts
- **Yellow/cream units**: Felsic volcanics or sedimentary sequences
- **White/pale areas**: Possibly alluvial/transported cover or sedimentary sequences, prominent in the central-eastern portions (possibly lake systems — consistent with playa lakes common in this region)
- **Olive/brown linear patterns**: Possibly banded iron formation (BIF) or metamorphic units

Multiple **linear structural features** (interpreted as faults or shear zones) are visible crossing the map in northeast and northwest orientations — these are shown as fine diagonal lines cutting across the geology and are characteristic of the regional structural architecture of the Murchison goldfields.

The tenement outline (bold black polygon) is centrally positioned and straddles what appears to be a greenstone belt corridor between granitic terranes — a classic Archaean lode gold exploration setting.

The **legend/title box** is visible in the lower right corner but detail is not fully legible at this resolution.

## Text and Labels
- **Title**: "Cuddingwarra Project" / "Tenement Group over GSWA 1:250 Geology"
- **Tenement identifiers**: EL 3000833, EL 3000742, E 2003097, P 2003098
- **Locality names**: Redgum Bell, Cuddingwarra, Great Find
- **Coordinate grid values** (MGA/AMG): Eastings and northings visible on margins — approximate values suggest MGA Zone 50:
  - Northings: ~6,971,000 N to ~7,025,000 N (approximately)
  - Eastings: ~700,000 E to ~740,000 E (approximately)
- **Legend box**: Lower right corner — labelled as tenement boundary description and geology source attribution (GSWA 1:250,000)

## Research Value
This figure establishes the **spatial relationship between the Cuddingwarra Project tenements and the regional Archaean greenstone geology**, demonstrating that the exploration licences are strategically positioned along or adjacent to greenstone belt lithologies in proximity to the historically significant **Redgum Bell gold deposit**. A geologist or research assistant would learn the tenure status of the project and its geological context — specifically that the tenements target the greenstone-granite contact zones and associated structural corridors that are the primary hosts to orogenic gold mineralisation in the Murchison. This provides the foundational spatial framework for interpreting all subsequent geochemical, geophysical, and drilling data reported in the WAMEX report.
